manifest.description: Sandbox provider plugin that provisions exe.dev VMs as Paperclip execution environments.
manifest.author: Paperclip

environmentDrivers.description: Provisions exe.dev VMs through the HTTPS API, then runs commands over direct SSH for long-lived Paperclip workloads.

apiKey.description: Paste your exe.dev API token, or pick a saved Paperclip secret. Create one at exe.dev → Settings → API tokens with `/exec` scope (`new`, `ls`, `rm`).

sshPrivateKey.description: Paste the SSH private key you registered with exe.dev, or pick a saved secret. Leave blank to fall back to an on-host key (see Advanced → SSH access).

sshUser.description: Login user on the VM. Leave blank to use the image default, usually `root`.

sshIdentityFile.description: Absolute path to a private key on the Paperclip host. Used only when SSH Private Key is empty.

sshPort.description: SSH port for direct VM access.

strictHostKeyChecking.description: Host key policy passed to ssh via StrictHostKeyChecking. Typical values are `accept-new`, `yes`, or `no`.

image.description: Optional container image to use when creating the VM.

cpu.description: Optional CPU count passed to `exe.dev new --cpu`.

command.description: Optional container command passed to `exe.dev new --command`.

env.description: Optional environment variables applied at VM creation time.

integrations.description: Optional exe.dev integrations to attach during VM creation.

tags.description: Optional tags to apply during VM creation.

setupScript.description: Optional first-boot setup script passed to `exe.dev new --setup-script`.

prompt.description: Optional Shelley prompt passed to `exe.dev new --prompt`.

comment.description: Optional short note attached to created VMs.

namePrefix.description: Optional prefix used when generating VM names.

apiUrl.description: Optional exe.dev HTTPS API base URL or /exec endpoint. Defaults to https://exe.dev/exec.

timeoutMs.description: Timeout for VM lifecycle and SSH operations in milliseconds.

reuseLease.description: Whether to keep the VM alive between runs instead of deleting it on release.
